_comment: Pure-Python packages NOT in the upstream Pyodide distribution that Chickadee needs in the one canonical Pyodide (Public/pyodide). scripts/add-pyodide-extras.py downloads each pinned wheel, verifies its sha256, drops it into Public/pyodide, and injects a pyodide-lock.json entry so loadPyodide({packages:[...]}) can load it. Re-run by scripts/setup-vendor.sh after every re-vendor so a Pyodide bump never silently drops these. All wheels must be py3/py2.py3-none-any (ABI-independent) so they survive a Pyodide ABI bump unchanged.
_mypy_deps_note: The upstream Pyodide 'mypy' package lists depends:[] in pyodide-lock.json, but mypy needs typing_extensions and mypy_extensions at runtime (mypy/build.py, mypy/errorcodes.py). typing-extensions is already in the base lock; mypy_extensions is not, so it is vendored here. Both are pulled in via nb_mypy's depends so they load whenever the editor kernel loads nb_mypy. Without them mypy.api.run() raises ModuleNotFoundError and the kernel's nb_mypy type checker errors.
_comm_note: pyodide_kernel imports `comm` at kernel startup (pyodide_kernel/comm.py -> kernel.py), but `comm` is in neither the upstream Pyodide lock nor the kernel's piplite all.json index. In the notebooks editor (which eagerly initializes the comm manager) the kernel's auto-install runs `piplite.install('comm')`, which fails with 'Can't find a pure Python 3 wheel for: comm' because disablePyPIFallback blocks the CDN. Vendoring it into the lock lets micropip resolve it locally (micropip checks the lock before the index).
